title: Operator TUI: Optional Carbonyl browser render mode for CenterViewport
summary: Add an explicit, user-triggered browser render mode for the Operator TUI center viewport. When enabled, the content that is currently shown in the middle/center view is transformed into a temporary browser document and rendered through Carbonyl inside the same CenterViewport region. This must not become the default rendering path.

existing_architecture_notes: CenterViewport currently owns the assigned middle-area rectangle and returns ViewportDrawRequest objects for payload rendering.
The documented visual renderer system already separates VisualView, Renderer, OutputAdapter, CapabilityDetector and FrameScheduler.
Markdown/Mermaid rendering is documented as a center-view mode with ANSI fallback and optional image rendering.
Carbonyl should be integrated as another explicit center render mode, not as a replacement for ANSI/Sixel/Kitty rendering.

non_goals: Do not make Carbonyl the default renderer.
Do not route arbitrary web URLs through Carbonyl unless explicitly requested by the user.
Do not allow the Carbonyl subprocess to write outside the CenterViewport region.
Do not remove the existing Markdown/Mermaid ANSI fallback path.
Do not require Carbonyl for normal Operator TUI startup.

desired_behavior.case: User explicitly switches current center content to browser mode
desired_behavior.behavior: The active center view is snapshotted, converted into an HTML document, and rendered by Carbonyl in the same CenterViewport bounds.
The Markdown source and Mermaid blocks are wrapped into a local HTML document. Mermaid rendering may use local bundled JS only; no remote CDN loading by default.

desired_behavior.case: Current center content is plain text/log/source
desired_behavior.behavior: The content is escaped and shown in a pre/code oriented HTML template.

desired_behavior.case: Current center content is already an HTML preview
desired_behavior.behavior: The HTML is loaded directly after sanitization or wrapped in the standard Carbonyl document shell.

desired_behavior.case: Carbonyl is missing or fails to start
desired_behavior.behavior: The TUI remains usable, shows a short degraded-state message in the center viewport, and offers the existing renderer fallback.
The Carbonyl subprocess is stopped, temporary files are cleaned up, input focus returns to the Operator TUI, and the previous VisualView state is restored.

commands.name: center.browser.toggle
commands.description: Toggle Carbonyl browser mode for the current CenterViewport content.

commands.name: center.browser.open_current
commands.description: Open the currently displayed center content in Carbonyl without changing global defaults.

commands.name: center.browser.exit
commands.description: Exit Carbonyl mode and restore the previous center view.

suggested_keybindings.reason: F8/F9/F10 are already documented for the view switcher; F7 is close but avoids the existing assignments.

suggested_keybindings.scope: Only while Carbonyl mode has focus.

implementation_tasks.title: Detect Carbonyl availability as a degraded optional capability
details: Add a capability flag named carbonyl_browser.
Detect the configured carbonyl_binary via PATH or explicit config path.
Do not fail TUI startup when Carbonyl is absent.
Expose availability and short reason in the view switcher/capability report.
acceptance_criteria: With Carbonyl installed, capability report marks carbonyl_browser available.
Without Carbonyl installed, Operator TUI starts normally and marks browser mode unavailable/degraded with a short reason.
No existing renderer capability changes behavior because of Carbonyl detection.

implementation_tasks.title: Add a CenterViewport content snapshot contract
details: Introduce a serializable CenterContentSnapshot with content_type, title, source_text, html_text, metadata and scroll position.
Every VisualView that can be opened in browser mode must expose get_browser_snapshot() or return an explicit unsupported reason.
Support at least markdown, mermaid-markdown, plain_text, ansi_text, source_code, html_preview and artifact_preview snapshots.
Snapshotting must not mutate the active VisualView state.
acceptance_criteria: The currently visible center content can be snapshotted before switching to Carbonyl mode.
Unsupported visual views show a clear message instead of crashing.
Scroll/title/context metadata survive switching into and out of browser mode.

implementation_tasks.title: Transform current center content into safe local HTML
details: Create a BrowserDocumentAdapter that converts CenterContentSnapshot into a complete HTML document.
Escape text by default and only allow trusted/sanitized HTML when content_type is html_preview.
Render Markdown via the existing Markdown/Mermaid pipeline where possible; otherwise use a simple local Markdown fallback.
Bundle minimal CSS locally to match the Ananta TUI theme without external network requests.
For Mermaid blocks, prefer local mermaid.min.js if present; otherwise display the diagram source plus a degraded reason.
acceptance_criteria: Plain text containing HTML/script characters is displayed escaped and cannot execute as HTML.
Markdown content gets headings, lists and code blocks rendered in Carbonyl mode.
Mermaid blocks do not fetch remote CDN content by default.
Generated HTML is deterministic enough for snapshot tests.

implementation_tasks.title: Run Carbonyl in a bounded PTY attached to CenterViewport
details: Start Carbonyl as a subprocess with terminal dimensions derived from the CenterViewport region.
Use a PTY so Carbonyl can behave like an interactive terminal app.
Clip/pad Carbonyl output to the CenterViewport rectangle before writing to terminal output.
Forward keyboard/mouse input only while browser mode owns focus.
On resize, propagate the new center dimensions to the PTY/subprocess.
Kill the subprocess on exit, TUI shutdown or timeout.
acceptance_criteria: Carbonyl renders only inside the center viewport and never overwrites header/footer/sidebar content.
Input focus is exclusive: browser mode gets keys while active; normal TUI gets keys after exit.
Resize does not crash and does not leave stale output outside the region.
Subprocess cleanup leaves no orphan Carbonyl process after exit.

implementation_tasks.title: Wire browser mode into Operator TUI commands and footer hints
details: Add explicit commands center.browser.toggle, center.browser.open_current and center.browser.exit.
Add F7 as suggested default only if there is no conflict in keybindings_config.py.
Footer/status line must show when browser mode is active and how to leave it.
Switching to browser mode must wrap the currently active center content, not open an empty browser page.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing the configured key on Markdown view opens the same Markdown content in Carbonyl mode.
Pressing exit restores the exact previous center view.
The mode cannot accidentally activate during normal TUI startup.
Keybinding conflict detection catches unsafe duplicate assignments.

implementation_tasks.title: Add safe defaults for local/remote browser rendering
details: Default to local generated file/data rendering only.
Disable remote URL loading unless allow_remote_urls is explicitly true.
Restrict generated temp files to configured temp_root and clean them after use.
Document that Carbonyl is a browser engine and must be treated as untrusted-content execution surface when remote URLs are enabled.
Add an optional offline mode flag that injects no remote scripts/assets.
acceptance_criteria: By default, opening current content in Carbonyl performs no remote network fetches caused by Ananta templates.
Remote URLs are rejected with a clear message unless explicitly allowed.
Temp files are created under the configured root and removed on normal exit.
Security docs explain local-only default and remote-risk tradeoff.

implementation_tasks.title: Add tests and a smoke command for browser mode
details: Add unit tests for snapshot conversion, HTML escaping, config parsing and missing Carbonyl fallback.
Add a smoke script that renders a Markdown file through the Carbonyl document adapter and optionally launches Carbonyl if available.
Mock subprocess execution for CI so Carbonyl is not required in default tests.
Add an integration test marker for local manual Carbonyl tests.
acceptance_criteria: Default CI passes without Carbonyl installed.
Manual smoke script prints a useful degraded message when Carbonyl is missing.
Manual smoke script can launch Carbonyl with a generated local HTML file when Carbonyl exists.
Viewport clipping tests cover Carbonyl output frames.

mermaid_architecture:
flowchart TD
    A[Active CenterViewport content] --> B[CenterContentSnapshot]
    B --> C[BrowserDocumentAdapter]
    C --> D[Safe local HTML file/data URL]
    D --> E[CarbonylRunner PTY subprocess]
    E --> F[BrowserModeController]
    F --> G[Clipped terminal frames inside CenterViewport]
    H[Keyboard/mouse input] -->|only while browser mode active| F
    I[Exit browser mode] --> J[Stop Carbonyl and restore previous VisualView]
acceptance_summary: Carbonyl mode is explicit-only and never the default.
The current center content is reused when switching into browser mode.
Missing Carbonyl causes graceful degradation, not startup failure.
Browser output is clipped to the CenterViewport.
Normal TUI input/rendering is restored after leaving browser mode.
Remote web loading is disabled by default and must be explicitly enabled.
